title: "DELETE FROM tbl" statements do not obtain a shared-cache write-lock on table "tbl"
description:
dan added on 2013-09-26 14:17:05:
In shared-cache mode, SQLite uses read and write locks on individual tables to isolate transactions from other shared-cache users. This means that if one shared-cache user opens a transaction and modifies the contents of a table, a write-lock is taken on that table to prevent other shared-cache clients from reading the modified data before it is committed. See section 2.2 of the following for details:

   http://www.sqlite.org/sharedcache.html

Currently, if the statement that writes to the table is a DELETE statement that uses the truncate optimization, this lock is not being obtained. Meaning a second shared-cache client is not prohibited from observing an empty table before the transaction is committed (like a read_uncommitted client would).

To clarify, this:

  BEGIN; DELETE FROM tbl WHERE 1;

obtains the correct shared-cache locks, but this:

  BEGIN; DELETE FROM tbl;

does not.
dan added on 2013-09-26 14:24:16:
Has existed since at least version 3.6.4.
bug_features_seed: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ COMMITTED;
key_bug_features: DELETE statements with truncate optimization do not obtain write-lock
